articles.title: HE SAID THIS?!
articles.description: Nothing is off limits in this edition of Ask Uncle Chael. In this fan-driven segment, Chael Sonnen takes on questions from across the fight world and delivers answers that are anything but predictable. From the reality of Jorge Masvidal returning to the UFC, to what actually separates the UFC from every other promotion, Chael breaks down topics with his usual mix of honesty and perspective. He also dives into how fight promotion really works, why resumes don’t matter the way people think they do, and what smaller creators get wrong when trying to build an audience. And as always, things don’t stay on track for long. From weight cutting strategies to wild hypotheticals, this episode covers everything from serious analysis to completely unexpected turns. Because when Chael is answering your questions, you never really know where it’s going next. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the questions fans actually want answered. #UFC #MMA #ChaelSonnen #AskUncleChael 0:00 Ask Uncle Chael Begins 0:30 Masvidal Not Returning 1:30 Why Fighters Don’t Leave on Their Terms 2:30 What UFC Does Better Than Everyone 4:00 Why Resumes Don’t Matter 5:30 RAF Contender Series Talk 6:30 Brutal Advice for Creators 8:30 Arman Situation Explained 10:00 Water Cutting Explained 12:00 Promo Work Never Ends 13:30 Bo Bassett Is the Real Deal Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: The Division Needs Prates to Win
articles.description: Carlos Prates is the betting favorite over Jack Della Maddalena And according to Chael Sonnen, that doesn’t add up. In this breakdown, Chael reacts to the odds and questions how this fight is being viewed. While Prates has momentum, Jack is fighting at home and has already proven himself at the highest level. The line feels off. But the bigger issue isn’t just the fight. It’s what happens after. Chael breaks down the current state of the welterweight division, including what’s next for Islam Makhachev and why there may not be a meaningful announcement coming. With potential matchups involving Ian Machado Garry or Michael Morales, the path forward lacks clarity. That’s where this fight matters. If Prates wins, the division moves forward with a new contender. If he loses, things stall. Because right now, there isn’t a clear direction. And that makes this line even more confusing. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that don’t add up. #UFC #MMA #CarlosPrates #JackDellaMaddalena #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Prates Is the Favorite 0:40 Why That’s Surprising 1:30 What’s Happening at 170 2:30 No Big Fight for Islam 3:30 Why This Fight Matters 4:30 What Happens If Prates Wins 5:30 What Happens If Jack Wins 6:30 The Division Gets Stuck 7:30 Nobody Knows What’s Next Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: The Real Risk for Chimaev…
articles.description: Darren Till supports Khamzat Chimaev. But he also raised a concern. In this breakdown, Chael Sonnen reacts to Till’s comments on the matchup with Sean Strickland and explains why one small detail could matter more than anything else. Because Till has been in the room with both guys. He has felt their pace. Their pressure. Their style. And while he believes Chimaev will be the bigger, stronger presence, he also pointed to something specific. If Chimaev uses too much energy early, this could become a very difficult fight. That stands out. Chael breaks down why fatigue is often misunderstood in MMA, and why being tired does not always mean a fighter is fading. But when multiple fighters who have trained with Chimaev bring up the same concern, it becomes harder to ignore. Especially against someone like Strickland. A fighter who keeps pushing, round after round. Because this fight may not come down to skill. It may come down to when the energy is spent. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the details that decide fights. #UFC #MMA #DarrenTill #KhamzatChimaev #SeanStrickland #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Darren Till Speaks on the Fight 0:45 Why His Opinion Matters 1:30 What Till Said About Strickland 2:30 The “Bigger, Stronger” Question 3:30 The Round One Warning 4:30 The Conditioning Narrative 5:30 Why Being Tired Doesn’t Matter 6:30 The Real Question About Chimaev 7:30 Why This Detail MattersBad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: The Problem with Ronda and MVP Overthrowing UFC
articles.description: The Problem with Ronda and MVP Ronda Rousey keeps saying the same thing. MVP is going to overthrow the UFC. And somehow, it’s working. In this breakdown, Chael Sonnen explains why this storyline has taken on a life of its own, even though the fundamentals don’t add up. Because when you really look at it, nothing about the fight is clear. We don’t know the weight class. We don’t know the rounds. We barely know the structure. And yet, people are debating a head-to-head battle between promotions like it’s real. That’s the interesting part. Chael breaks down how Ronda has created headlines without relying on the fight itself, and why that might be more powerful than traditional promotion. At the same time, he questions whether the model can actually hold together, especially with one-off deals, uncertain talent, and no long-term structure. So what are we really watching? A real shift in the sport… or just a great story being told? Because right now, both things seem possible. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the narratives that don’t quite add up. #UFC #MMA #RondaRousey #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Ronda Says It Again0:40 Why Repeating It Works1:30 MVP vs UFC Narrative2:30 We Don’t Know the Fight3:30 Why This Is So Strange4:30 Ronda’s Real Impact5:30 Why MVP Model Is Risky6:30 This Doesn’t Add Up7:30 But It’s Still Working Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Sean Strickland Crossed the Line
articles.description: Sean Strickland just took things too far. In this breakdown, Chael Sonnen reacts to Strickland’s comments about Khamzat Chimaev and explains why this situation is different from normal fight promotion. Because this is no longer just talk. Chael breaks down how quickly these situations can become real, especially when there is history, tension, and the possibility of fighters running into each other outside of the cage. In a sport where confrontation is part of the build, there is still a line. And this crossed it. Drawing from his own experience with heated rivalries and real-world altercations, Chael explains why introducing a weapon changes everything. What might start as promotion can quickly become something much more serious. The message is simple. Do everything else. Build the fight. Create the moment. Just don’t bring a gun into it. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the moments that go too far. #UFC #MMA #SeanStrickland #KhamzatChimaev #ChaelSonnen 0:00 What Strickland Said 0:40 Why This Crosses the Line 1:30 This Could Actually Happen 2:30 Chael’s Brazil Story 4:00 The Problem With Bringing a Gun 5:30 Why This Changes Everything 6:30 Promotion vs Reality 7:30 Just Don’t Bring a Gun Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Conor McGregor Said Yes to Carlos Prates
articles.description: Conor McGregor already had his next fight. According to Chael Sonnen, the matchup was set. The opponent was Carlos Prates. And Conor said yes. So what happened? In this breakdown, Chael explains how quickly things can shift in the fight business, and why the biggest opportunities are often the hardest to hold onto. Despite accepting the fight, McGregor never made it to the Octagon with Prates, and the conversation quietly disappeared. That matters. Because right now, at 170 pounds, the biggest opportunity is not a title fight. It is Conor McGregor. Chael breaks down why fighters should be calling for that fight, how one moment on the microphone can change everything, and why nobody in this current matchup is taking advantage of the opportunity in front of them. The blueprint is simple. Win the fight. Call out Conor. But if nobody says it, nothing happens. Because in this division, there is one prize bigger than the belt. And it is still Conor McGregor. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that almost happened. #UFC #MMA #ConorMcGregor #CarlosPrates #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Who Is Conor Fighting Next 0:45 Why Rumors Usually Mean Something 1:30 The Max Holloway Clue 2:30 Conor Accepted the Prates Fight 3:30 What Went Wrong 4:30 Why This Isn’t About a Title 5:30 Red Panty Night Is Still Real6 :30 Why Nobody Is Calling Him Out 7:30 The Opportunity Being Missed Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Carlos Ulberg CALLS OUT Alex Pereira after BEATING Jiri Prochazka for UFC belt | Daniel Cormier Show
articles.description: Daniel Cormier is joined by new UFC Light Heavyweight champion Carlos Ulberg, where he talks about wanting to fight Alex Pereira after beating Jiri Prochazka to win the vacant Light Heavyweight belt. Alex is moving up to heavyweight to fight Ciryl Gane at the White House in June, but Ulberg wants Alex after that fight as he has been the king of the Light Heavyweight division. Carlos says that in order to be “the man,” you have to beat “the man,” and that is Alex Pereira. All lines provided by @hardrockbet Follow me on all social media platforms! articles.title: Enough With “Accidental” Eye Pokes #ufc
articles.description: Eye pokes have been a problem in MMA for years—and the current system isn’t fixing it. In this clip, the debate centers around accountability. Jorge Masvidal suggested taking a percentage of a fighter’s purse for eye pokes—but does that really work? When fighters are paid differently, a percentage penalty doesn’t hit equally. One fighter could lose $100,000… while the other barely feels it. So what’s the real solution? A harsher one: disqualification. If a fighter commits an eye poke, intentional or not, they’re responsible. Hands don’t move on their own. And if the consequence is severe enough—losing the fight and a major portion of the purse—it changes behavior immediately. This isn’t about punishment—it’s about prevention. The current warning system hasn’t stopped the issue. Stronger consequences might. Because at the highest level of the sport, fighters should be accountable for everything they do inside the cage. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real fight game insight. #JorgeMasvidal #UFC #MMA #EyePokes #FightRules #MMANews #CombatSports

articles.title: Gable Steveson Talk Is Bad for Aspinall #ufc
articles.description: Tom Aspinall doesn’t have a skill problem—he has a perception problem. In this clip, the spotlight shifts to how quickly narratives can change in MMA. When Tyron Woodley suggests that Gable Steveson could not only beat Aspinall but dominate him, it creates a ripple effect. Suddenly, a fighter who hasn’t even stepped into the UFC octagon is part of the conversation—and that’s a dangerous place for a champion to be. Not long ago, many believed Aspinall could beat anyone alive, even Jon Jones. That was the narrative. That was the perception. But in this sport, perception doesn’t stay still—it shifts fast. And silence doesn’t help. In a game where attention equals power, not responding doesn’t protect your position—it risks losing it. When new names start taking your spotlight, the story begins to change whether you like it or not. This isn’t about what’s true—it’s about what people believe. And right now, that belief is being tested. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real insight into the fight game. #TomAspinall #GableSteveson #TyronWoodley #JonJones #UFC #MMA #FightTalk

articles.title: Why is Jack Della Maddalena the underdog?
articles.description: According to Chael Sonnen, it does not add up. In this breakdown, Chael reacts to the betting line favoring Carlos Prates and questions how the narrative around this fight has shifted so quickly. The explanation seems simple. Recency bias. Jack is coming off a rough loss. Carlos is coming off a big win. But Chael argues that is not enough. He walks through the matchup and challenges the logic behind the odds. A former world champion, fighting in his home territory, against a striker who has not faced that level of competition. When you actually lay out the details, the conclusion does not match the betting line. And that leads to a bigger point. Chael explains how the MMA community tends to build meaning around main events that is not really there. Every fight becomes a title eliminator. Every winner becomes a contender. Even when it does not make sense. Because when you really break this one down, there is one thing that becomes clear. People are saying something they do not actually believe. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that don’t add up. #UFC #MMA #JackDellaMaddalena #CarlosPrates #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Jack vs Prates Odds Surprise0:40 “You’re Only as Good as Last Fight”1:30 Why This Doesn’t Add Up2:30 The Carlos Problem3:30 Recency Bias Explained4:30 Jack’s Loss vs Islam5:30 Are We Undervaluing Jack?6:30 What Do the Odds Really Mean7:30 Stop Calling This a Title Fight Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Nobody Wants to Fight Gable Steveson
articles.description: Gable Steveson is ready for his UFC debut. The problem is finding someone willing to face him. In this breakdown, Chael Sonnen explains why the conversation around Gable’s first matchup is more complicated than it should be. It is not about skill. It is not about hype. It is about the system. Chael breaks down how the UFC rankings were designed to create opportunity, but are now being used as protection. Fighters rely on their ranking to avoid risk instead of chasing it. And that leaves a prospect like Gable stuck in the middle. Too dangerous to ignore. Too unproven to justify the risk. That creates a standoff. Chael compares it to past prospects who faced the same issue, where no ranked fighter wanted to take the fight, and the system had no way to properly move them forward. So what happens next? According to Chael, the UFC has two choices. Either fast-track Gable into a fight with a ranked opponent, or risk stalling his momentum before it ever starts. Because right now, there is one simple solution. All someone has to do is say yes. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the matchups that never get made. #UFC #MMA #GableSteveson #ChaelSonnen 0:00 The Gable Steveson Problem 0:40 Why Rankings Don’t Work 2:00 Rankings Used as Protection 3:30 Nobody Wants This Fight 5:00 Build a Fighter Is a Myth 6:30 Just Give Him a Ranked Opponent 8:00 The Risk No One Will Take Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Khamzat FIRES BACK at Strickland 😳 “He’d Be DEAD Already”

articles.title: Masvidal Is Right… This Should Be a Loss
articles.description: Jorge Masvidal has an idea. Take 20 percent of a fighter’s purse for an eye poke. According to Chael Sonnen, the idea is right. The execution is not. In this breakdown, Chael explains why eye pokes remain one of the most frustrating and dangerous problems in the sport, and why they have never been properly addressed. While Masvidal’s proposal focuses on financial penalties, Chael argues that the issue goes deeper. Because not all penalties are equal. A percentage fine creates uneven consequences depending on how much a fighter earns. And that raises a bigger question about fairness and consistency. But more importantly, Chael challenges the idea that eye pokes are truly accidental. From his perspective, fighters are responsible for where their hands go. Intent does not change the outcome. So what is the solution? Chael makes the case for stronger enforcement, including disqualifications if necessary. Because in a sport with very few rules, the ones that exist have to matter. Don’t touch the eyes. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the rules that define the fight. #UFC #MMA #JorgeMasvidal #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Masvidal’s 20% Eye Poke Rule 0:40 Why Eye Pokes Matter 1:30 The Problem With Fines 2:30 Why It Isn’t Equal 3:30 Accidental Doesn’t Matter 4:45 Fighters Are Responsible 5:45 Why DQ Makes More Sense 6:45 Officials Are Getting It Wrong 8:00 The Only Rules That Matter Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Why Adesanya Is #1 All-Time #ufc
articles.description: Israel Adesanya isn’t just a great fighter—he might be the greatest champion in UFC history. And the reason comes down to one thing: he mastered every part of the game. In this clip, the “Izzy formula” gets broken down. There are three moments every fighter has to capture—on the way to the cage, during the fight, and after it’s over. Most fighters hit one. A rare few hit two. But Adesanya? He checked all three boxes, every time. From unforgettable walkouts to elite-level performances under pressure, to sharp, memorable moments on the mic—Izzy understood that fighting isn’t just about winning. It’s about connecting, entertaining, and leaving an impression. That’s what separates champions from superstars. And when you combine both? You get something very rare. This isn’t just about skills—it’s about complete command of the spotlight. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real insight into what makes a true great. #IsraelAdesanya #UFC #MMA #FightTalk #GOAT #UFCMiddleweight #CombatSports

articles.title: This Prediction Sounds Crazy, But Hear Me Out #ufc
articles.description: By the end of 2026, Chase Hooper as heavyweight champion? It sounds wild—but there’s a real argument behind it. In this clip, the focus shifts to the psychological side of fighting—and how it can change everything. Tom Aspinall is one of the most dangerous heavyweights in the world, but when outside voices start influencing decisions—especially advice like “don’t fight”—that creates doubt. And doubt at the highest level? That’s a problem you can’t afford. Fights at this level are razor thin. Confidence, mindset, and willingness matter just as much as skill. If Aspinall isn’t fully locked in, even for a moment, that edge disappears. Meanwhile, chaos creates opportunity. And if the right matchups fall into place, someone like Hooper—who thrives in unpredictable situations—could find himself in a position nobody expected. Add in names like Alex Pereira potentially entering the mix, and suddenly the heavyweight division becomes wide open. It sounds crazy—until it happens. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold predictions, and real insight into the fight game. #ChaseHooper #TomAspinall #AlexPereira #UFC #MMA #Heavyweight #FightTalk

articles.description: Carlos Ulberg, the UFC's new light heavyweight champion after his knockout win vs. Jiri Prochazka, makes HIS DEBUT on the Daniel Cormier show! Ulberg talks about his upbringing in New Zealand and how being in foster care during his childhood helped shape the man and champion he is today. The champ gets into the viral comments made by Jiri Prochazka following the title fight at UFC 327 in Miami, and why he wasn't in his right state of mind when he called him out and said he "showed mercy." Carlos also reveals why he ended his rugby career short and eventually decided he'd take on fighting full time. And don't miss Ulberg talk about a potential fight with Alex Pereira, his ACL injury and the rehab process, and so much more! articles.title: Ronda Rousey Is Winning Without Fighting
articles.description: Ronda Rousey is controlling a fight she is not even in. In this breakdown, Chael Sonnen reacts to the back and forth between Ronda and Kayla Harrison, and explains why it matters more than it should. Because this is not about them fighting. It is about attention. Chael breaks down how Ronda created a narrative that pulled Kayla into the conversation, even though Kayla did not need to respond. With two Olympic gold medals and a championship career, Kayla had nothing to gain from engaging. But she did. Meanwhile, the actual fight with Gina Carano continues to lack energy, story, and urgency. Chael points out that Gina has done very little to build interest, leaving a vacuum that Ronda has filled on her own. And that is the difference. Some fighters wait for the moment. Others create it. Ronda is creating it. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that are built outside the cage. #UFC #MMA #RondaRousey #KaylaHarrison #chaelsonnenbad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Bo Bassett Is on Another Level
articles.description: Bo Bassett is doing something that almost never happens. In this breakdown, Chael Sonnen reacts to Bassett’s performance at the U.S. Open and explains why this is more than just another young prospect rising through the ranks. From winning titles to dominating with technical falls, Bassett is creating separation from the field in a way that is hard to ignore. Chael compares what he is seeing to rare examples from the past, including names like Aaron Pico, but even those comparisons come with limits. What makes Bassett different is not just the wins. It is the volume, the willingness to compete, and the consistency across styles and levels. Because that matters. Chael explains why the best competitors are the ones who show up again and again, entering every tournament they can and proving themselves in real time. It is not just about talent. It is about mindset. And that is where Bassett stands out. This is not hype. This is something you can see happening right now.
